narrative: OMX30 opens to a mixed risk-off session despite remaining +6.7% above the 200-day MA in established bull trend. US yields surged 82bp overnight to 4.661%, creating meaningful headwinds for tech and real estate valuations, while weakness across NASDAQ (-0.72%), Asia (Hang Seng -1.11%), and commodities signals broad deleveraging. The weak ADX reading (13.2, below 18) severely dampens confidence in sustained directional moves — technical support erodes to ~30% credibility. SEK strength (both crosses down) offers minor tailwind for export-heavy industrials like Volvo and Atlas Copco, but copper's -1.16% drop and oil's -1.20% decline hit mining and industrial cost structures. Defensives (Essity, AstraZeneca) may benefit from modest risk-off rotation, while banks face compression from yield surge despite NIM gains.
morningBrief: OMX30 tracking lower in early European session as Asia stumbles and Treasury yields spike. Watch 3123 (yesterday close) as immediate support; a break below 3090 (lower Bollinger band) signals deeper consolidation in what remains a weak ADX environment (13.2). Sector calls: Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) consolidate yield gains but lack conviction; Industrials (Volvo, Atlas Copco) supported by SEK strength but pressured by commodity weakness—hold tactical longs only above 3100. Mining (Boliden) and Materials (SSAB) flagged for weakness on copper/oil decline; no reversal setup yet. Tech (Ericsson, Hexagon) vulnerable to NASDAQ spillover and higher discount rates. Pharma/Essity appeal as defensive rotations if risk-off accelerates. Key catalyst: US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET—if weak, expect further yield pressure and OMX30 downside. Range-trade bias until ADX recovers above 18.
drivers: Weak global sentiment: NASDAQ -0.72%, Hang Seng -1.11%, Nikkei -0.97%
Rising US yields (10Y +0.82%) compress tech/real estate valuations
Weak ADX (13.2) reduces directional conviction; RSI 47.53 neutral
SEK strength (EUR/SEK -0.63%, USD/SEK -0.59%) mixed for exporters
Commodity weakness: Oil -1.20%, Copper -1.16% pressure industrials & mining

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +0.82% to 4.661% widens NIM expectations; however, weak ADX (13.2) and risk-off sentiment cap upside conviction.
Oil -1.20% and copper -1.16% pressure input costs; SEK strength (+0.63% vs EUR/SEK) offers minor offset but insufficient to overcome commodity headwinds. ADX weakness (13.2) reduces confidence.
NASDAQ -0.72% overnight; US 10Y yield +0.82% compresses DCF valuations. Risk-off sentiment spillover from Asia (Hang Seng -1.11%).
Copper -1.16% and gold -0.81% eliminate upside catalysts. Oil weakness (-1.20%) compounds industrial headwinds.
US 10Y yield +0.82% raises refinancing costs and discount rates. Risk-off sentiment typical headwind.
Risk-off backdrop and defensive rotation favor defensive assets. Modest gains vs. broader market weakness.
Weak EUR/SEK (-0.63%) raises H&M sourcing costs; broader consumer spending pressure from yield rise and risk-off.

watchList.reason: Yield surge (+0.82%) supports NIM expansion; but weak ADX and risk-off sentiment limit upside. Scale into strength above 3130 OMX level.
watchList.catalyst: Jobless Claims 14:30 CET; ECB rate expectations

watchList.reason: NASDAQ -0.72% overnight; higher yields compress valuations. Tech sector under pressure. No reversal signal until ADX >18.
Copper -1.16% and gold -0.81% eliminate key drivers. No supportive catalysts in current environment.
Defensive positioning in risk-off environment; modest upside if VIX extends higher or equity selloff accelerates.
Weak EUR/SEK (-0.63%) pressures sourcing margins; consumer discretionary weak in risk-off backdrop.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET — key market mover. No major Swedish releases. Watch ECB speakers for rate guidance.
topRisk: Further yield surge (10Y >4.70%) triggers tech/real estate washout and broader deleveraging. Weak ADX (13.2) means limited institutional conviction to support bounces; cascade risk elevated if OMX30 breaks 3090.
technicalSummary: Weak momentum: MACD below signal (hist=-6.595), RSI 47.53 neutral, ADX 13.2 (very low conviction)
Bollinger %B=0.36 in lower half signals mild downside bias
Stochastic K=33.5, D=27.5 — neutral, no mean reversion signal yet
52-week: 80% of range, -5.0% from high — near recent lows, support structure critical
